violations.text: Staff was observed scooping ice with a beverage class. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (salad greens) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Affected foods disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Raw animal foods (liquid eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cheeses) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine, slicer, stand mixer bowl, dough roller) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. All items taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

Pork was observed held above fill line in prep unit at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Pork out of temperature control was disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
House-made tomato jam and apple butter were observed with dates exceeding 7 days. PIC disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

Working containers of dry and liquid ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. PIC labeled containers during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

The door to the outside, located at the hostess desk, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The ice scoop was stored in the bar ice bin with handle in contact with ice. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Inspector place scoop so that handle was exposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025

The following were observed in poor repair: -prep unit door gaskets -thermometer cover held with tape on upstairs prep unit -upstairs freezer door (insulation exposed). New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2025

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. Inspector provided. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the basement knife rack and the stand mixers have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
Hot water was not available at the bar 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025

The sink used for food preparation is lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2025
The outside dumpsters are not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 01/23/2025
The ceiling in the basement prep area was observed with peeling/cracked paint. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025
